narrative: Fed’s Williams signals a higher-for-longer regime as PCE inflation tops 4%, anchoring USD strength despite a daily DXY retreat; gold higher +0.03%; equities -0.01%; DXY -0.15%; VIX 18.9. USD/JPY holds at 161.76 as the 275bp Fed-BoJ carry persists, though moderate put skew in 25d RR suggests hedging against a potential Tokyo inflation surprise. NZD/USD at 0.5647 remains the week's dominant G10 bilateral theme, driven by a 150bp Fed-RBNZ carry advantage and a 2.269% G10 performance spread. Watch Tokyo CPI at 23:30 UTC for JPY directional risk.
